criteria.justification: The Route behavior section says the handler accepts only `{ "body": string }` and returns `201` with exactly `id`, `body`, `authorId`, and `createdAt` with no envelope.
The backend-notes Deviations section explicitly says no contract deviation was introduced and that any future envelope change would require an architecture update instead of a silent backend change.
The Files written list contains only `server/**` files plus `docs/features/team-comments/backend-notes.md`, with no `client/**` changes.
The simulated `backend-notes.md` section summarizes what was implemented against the plan, states there were no deviations, and records the path-boundary note.

The Compatibility / governance notes call the `Role` drop destructive and non-backward-compatible, and they require a migration that backfills existing documents from legacy `Role` values into the new enum.
The Files written list is limited to `server/models/User.js`, `server/services/userRoleService.js`, and `docs/features/user-roles-rework/backend-notes.md`.
The simulated `backend-notes.md` spells out what was implemented, notes there was no contract drift, and explains the migration/backfill needed for existing documents.
